Party_Details.Name: Lim Seng Choon David

Facts.Text: Meeting between Lim Seng Choon David and Mr Gary Anthony Hogg where early retirement was discussed.
stated oral agreement made between Lim Seng Choon David and the defendants regarding early retirement terms.
Lim Seng Choon David claims entitlement to $273,550 and $188,100 for unutilised holiday or leave entitlement and 'earned leaves'.
Claim for bonus of $30,692 for the year 2013.
Claim for $228,000 for six months’ salary.
Claim for purchase of shares in Global Maritime Group at the price of Norwegian Kroner 249,899.70.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether an oral agreement was formed between the plaintiff and the defendants regarding early retirement terms.
Party_Details.Rule: An oral agreement requires clear evidence of mutual intent to create legal obligations.
Party_Details.Application: The plaintiff stated that an oral agreement was reached during the meeting on 24 November 2014. The defendants argued that the discussions were mere negotiations and not binding.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found no evidence of a binding oral agreement, as the discussions were deemed to be negotiations without mutual intent to create legal obligations.

Party_Details.Name: Global Maritime Holdings Ltd

Facts.Text: Global Maritime Holdings Ltd is the parent company of Global Maritime Consultancy Pte Ltd.
Mr Gary Anthony Hogg, director of Global Maritime Holdings Ltd, met with Lim Seng Choon David to discuss early retirement.
Global Maritime Holdings Ltd denies the existence of an oral agreement with Lim Seng Choon David.
Draft Separation Agreement and Share Purchase Agreement circulated to Lim Seng Choon David.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the company is legally responsible for obligations under an stated oral agreement for early retirement.
Party_Details.Rule: A company is bound by an agreement only if there is clear evidence of mutual consent to the terms.
Party_Details.Application: The defendants argued that the discussions were subject to contract and no binding agreement was reached. The plaintiff stated that an oral agreement was formed during the meeting.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court concluded that the evidence did not support the existence of a binding oral agreement, as the discussions were considered negotiations.

Party_Details.Name: Global Maritime Consultancy Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Global Maritime Consultancy Pte Ltd is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Global Maritime Holdings Ltd.
Mr Gary Anthony Hogg, director of Global Maritime Consultancy Pte Ltd, met with Lim Seng Choon David to discuss early retirement.
Global Maritime Consultancy Pte Ltd denies the existence of an oral agreement with Lim Seng Choon David.
Draft Separation Agreement and Share Purchase Agreement circulated to Lim Seng Choon David.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the company is legally responsible for obligations under an stated oral agreement for early retirement.
Party_Details.Rule: A company is bound by an agreement only if there is clear evidence of mutual consent to the terms.
Party_Details.Application: The defendants argued that the discussions were subject to contract and no binding agreement was reached. The plaintiff stated that an oral agreement was formed during the meeting.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court concluded that the evidence did not support the existence of a binding oral agreement, as the discussions were considered negotiations.

Party_Details.Name: Global Maritime Consultancy Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Lim Seng Choon David made trips to Hong Kong and China at the company's expense without business operations there.
Claims for personal expenses such as passport renewal and home internet were made by Lim Seng Choon David.
Lim Seng Choon David issued cheques to his wife for cleaning services without proper contracts.
Lim Seng Choon David authorised payments for used furniture and appliances from his wife.
Lease agreements were entered into with properties owned by Lim Seng Choon David's family.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the director contravened his management obligations by engaging in transactions that presented conflicts of interest.
Party_Details.Rule: Directors must avoid conflicts of interest and act in the best interests of the company.
Party_Details.Application: The plaintiff-in-counterclaim argued that the defendant-in-counterclaim engaged in transactions that were not in the company's best interests. The defendant-in-counterclaim denied any contravention of obligations.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the defendant-in-counterclaim breached his duties by engaging in transactions that presented conflicts of interest and were not in the company's best interests.

Party_Details.Name: Lim Seng Choon David

Facts.Text: Lim Seng Choon David made trips to Hong Kong and China at the company's expense without business operations there.
Claims for personal expenses such as passport renewal and home internet were made by Lim Seng Choon David.
Lim Seng Choon David issued cheques to his wife for cleaning services without proper contracts.
Lim Seng Choon David authorised payments for used furniture and appliances from his wife.
Lease agreements were entered into with properties owned by Lim Seng Choon David's family.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the director contravened his management obligations by engaging in transactions that presented conflicts of interest.
Party_Details.Rule: Directors must avoid conflicts of interest and act in the best interests of the company.
Party_Details.Application: The defendant-in-counterclaim argued that his actions were in the company's best interests and were authorised. The plaintiff-in-counterclaim stated that the transactions were not beneficial to the company.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the defendant-in-counterclaim breached his duties by engaging in transactions that presented conflicts of interest and were not in the company's best interests.
